title: Angular resolver Options Schema
description: Generates a new, generic resolver definition in the given project.

name.description: The name of the new resolver.
name.title: Angular Resolver Options Schema
name.description: Creates a new resolver in your project. Resolvers are used to pre-fetch data before a route is activated, ensuring that the necessary data is available before the component is displayed. This can improve the user experience by preventing delays and loading states. This schematic generates a new resolver with the specified name and options.
The name for the new resolver. This will be used to create the resolver's class and spec files (e.g., `my-resolver.resolver.ts` and `my-resolver.resolver.spec.ts`).
name.x-prompt: What name would you like to use for the resolver?

skipTests.description: Do not create "spec.ts" test files for the new resolver.
Skip the generation of a unit test file `spec.ts` for the new resolver.

flat.description: When true (the default), creates the new files at the top level of the current project.
Creates the new resolver files at the top level of the current project. If set to false, a new folder with the resolver's name will be created to contain the files.

functional.description: Creates the resolver as a function `ResolveFn` instead of a class. Functional resolvers can be simpler for basic scenarios.

path.description: The path at which to create the interface that defines the resolver, relative to the current workspace.
The path where the resolver files should be created, relative to the current workspace. If not provided, the resolver will be created in the current directory.

project.description: The name of the project where the resolver should be created. If not specified, the CLI will determine the project from the current directory.
